channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Mamdani says New York City can't arrest Netanyahu but the federal government should
item.description: New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.
item.content:encoded:
New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.

(Image credit: Anna Connors/Anna Connors)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Smithsonian too focused on 'oppressors and the oppressed,' say House Republicans
item.description: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.
item.content:encoded:
In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Colorado wildfire victims return to their homes for the first time
item.description: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.content:encoded: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.dc:creator: Briana Heaney

item.title: Man accused of setting a fire outside a federal building in New York City
item.description: Authorities detained a former U.S. Army soldier who is accused of starting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside of a federal building in New York City that houses an immigration court.
item.content:encoded: Authorities detained a former U.S. Army soldier who is accused of starting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside of a federal building in New York City that houses an immigration court.
item.dc:creator: Gwynne Hogan

item.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How soccer brings a Bhutanese community together in Harrisburg, Pa.
item.description: Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.
item.content:encoded:
Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.

(Image credit: Maansi Srivastava)
item.dc:creator: Maansi Srivastava

item.title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
item.description: Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.
item.content:encoded:
Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.

(Image credit: Andi Rice for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: A homeless man was charged with a felony for camping. He's one of the first
item.description: When Joseph Lamont Williams refused to leave the public park where he has lived for years, he was charged with a felony. Camping bans are on the rise as states seek to crack down on homelessness.
item.content:encoded:
When Joseph Lamont Williams refused to leave the public park where he has lived for years, he was charged with a felony. Camping bans are on the rise as states seek to crack down on homelessness.

(Image credit: Erica Lynn for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marissa J. Lang

item.title: Arizona primaries show GOP voters backing Trump's picks for key posts
item.description: Trump-endorsed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race. Arizona Democrats choose who can try to flip that Republican-held House seat.
item.content:encoded:
Trump-endorsed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race. Arizona Democrats choose who can try to flip that Republican-held House seat. 

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Colorado wildfire victims return to their homes for the first time
item.description: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.content:encoded: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.dc:creator: Briana Heaney
